corpus.title: Canadian wildfire smoke pushes Toronto air quality to world's worst, reaches US
corpus.signal_text:
Canadian wildfire smoke pushes Toronto air quality to world's worst, reaches US

Toronto recorded among the worst air quality of all major cities worldwide on Wednesday as thick smoke from wildfires in Ontario blanketed its skyline and drifted into the US, prompting health warnings.

corpus.title: Toronto choked by wildfire smoke, records world's worst air quality
corpus.signal_text:
Toronto choked by wildfire smoke, records world's worst air quality
Despite the poor conditions, some residents continued to spend time outdoors, while others wore face masks as the smoke drifted across the city. The deteriorating air quality coincided with a heat alert, with temperatures reaching 33C, close to the record for 15 July. Authorities cancelled outdoor children's programmes and opened cooling centres, while Environment Canada warned that smoky conditions were unlikely to ease before Friday. Howard Shapiro, Toronto's Associate Medical Officer of Health, compared exposure to the smoke to passive smoking, warning that the fine particles contain a mixture of harmful chemicals capable of affecting anyone, regardless of age or underlying health. The smoke originated from 148 active wildfires burning across northwestern Ontario, where firefighters continue to battle multiple large blazes.

corpus.title: Toronto engulfed by wildfire smoke as US cities threatened
corpus.signal_text:
Toronto engulfed by wildfire smoke as US cities threatened

Monitor ranks Toronto as having the worst air quality on earth, surpassing Kinshasa, DR Congo, and New Delhi, India.

corpus.title: Wildfire smoke engulfs Toronto, Canada and turns its skies orange

Wildfire smoke turned skies orange over Toronto and the rest of Ontario.

corpus.title: 100 Million Americans Could Soon Face Smoky Skies Thanks To Canada
corpus.signal_text:
100 Million Americans Could Soon Face Smoky Skies Thanks To Canada

Heavy smoke from Canadian wildfires is predicted to spread across the United States from Wednesday to Friday, causing potential air pollution that could impact 100 million people. corpus.title: Incendies de Fontainebleau : le combat contre les flammes continue
corpus.signal_text:
Incendies de Fontainebleau : le combat contre les flammes continue

Des reprises de feu ont été signalées dans la forêt de Fontainebleau et les opérations des pompiers se sont poursuivies toute la journée, mercredi 15 juillet. Les centaines de personnes évacuées commencent progressivement à rentrer chez elles et à découvrir l'ampleur des dégâts.

corpus.title: Why is the sky yellow in northeastern US? Canadian wildfire smoke triggers air quality alerts in Massachusetts

Thick Canadian wildfire smoke has turned skies yellow over Boston and the Northeast, prompting Massachusetts to issue an air quality alert through Thursday.

corpus.title: La ville de Toronto devient irrespirable, sous la fumée des feux de forêt

La fumée émanant des incendies en cours dans le nord-ouest de l’Ontario a plongé la métropole dans un épais nuage de pollution, poussant les autorités à appeler les habitants à ne pas sortir. L’est des Etats-Unis est également affecté.
